hooks.command:
BASEDIR=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." 2>/dev/null && pwd || echo "$PWD"); MEMDIR="$BASEDIR/workspace"; content=''; for agent_dir in "$MEMDIR"/*/; do mem="${agent_dir}MEMORY.md"; [ -f "$mem" ] && content="$content--- $(basename "$agent_dir") ---
$(cat "$mem")
"; done; [ -z "$content" ] && exit 0; printf '%s' "$content" | jq -Rsc '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"SessionStart",additionalContext:.}}'

jq -nc '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"UserPromptSubmit",additionalContext:"ENFORCED RULES:
1. No Assumptions (Rule 003): Before claiming anything, search 5 locations (src/, public/, workspace/, data/, *.md). Every claim needs file:line proof.
2. Honesty (Rule 004): Do not say done if not done. Verify before reporting.
3. No Destructive Tests (Rule 001): Never use real IDs in DELETE/PUT operations.
4. Clean Up (Rule 002): Leave no garbage after every task."}}'

# SSOT_PROMPT_REMINDER
jq -nc '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"ENFORCED RULES:
1. No destructive tests with real data (Rule 001): Never use real workspace IDs, agent IDs, or production data in DELETE or PUT tests. Always use temporary or mock IDs for
2. Clean up after every task (Rule 002): After every task, clean up temporary files, unused variables, debug logs, and test artifacts. Leaving garbage creates te
3. No assumptions — verify before claiming (Rule 003): Never claim something is true without evidence. Before saying "X doesn't exist" or "Y is unused," search for it. Every c
4. Honesty — don't say done if it's not done (Rule 004): If a task isn't complete, don't say it is. If something is broken, admit it. If you're not sure, say so.

Every tick, ev
5. Response audit — block unverified claims (Rule 005): Every response is audited after completion. If it contains unverified claims, the response is blocked and must be rewrit
6. No sloppy work — think before you write (Rule 006): Think before every output. Don't rush. Don't write the first thing that comes to mind. Don't respond without reading.

-
7. Push requires runtime verification — syntax check alone is not enough (Rule 007): Before committing and pushing any change to a repository, the change must be verified to work at runtime. `node -c` synt"}}'

input=$(cat); fp=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_response.filePath // .tool_input.file_path // empty"); ns=$(echo "$input" | jq -r ".tool_input.new_string // empty"); if [ -z "$fp" ] || [ -z "$ns" ]; then exit 0; fi; if [ ! -f "$fp" ]; then jq -nc --arg f "$fp" '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"PostToolUse",additionalContext:("WARNING: File does not exist on disk! " + $f)}}'; exit 0; fi; sample=$(echo "$ns" | head -1 | sed 's/[^a-zA-Z0-9_ ]//g' | head -c 40); if [ -n "$sample" ] && ! grep -qF "$sample" "$fp" 2>/dev/null; then jq -nc --arg f "$fp" --arg s "$sample" '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"PostToolUse",additionalContext:("WARNING: Edit may not have applied. Expected text not found in " + $f + ": " + $s)}}'; fi

# SSOT_READ_TRACKER — Track read files for RULE 020 enforcement
fp=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty'); sid=$(jq -r '.session_id // "default"'); sf="/tmp/anuki-read-files-$sid"; if [ -n "$fp" ]; then echo "$fp" >> "$sf"; sort -u -o "$sf" "$sf"; fi

cmd=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*(pkill|killall)[[:space:]]"; then jq -nc '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"PreToolUse",additionalContext:"BLOCKED: pkill/killall is dangerous. Use /api/safe-restart or /api/agents/:id/stop."}}'; exit 2; fi; if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "rm\s+(-rf?\s+)?(/|~|\$HOME)"; then jq -nc '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"PreToolUse",additionalContext:"BLOCKED: Destructive rm on home/root directory."}}'; exit 2; fi

# SSOT_RULE_010 — Block destructive DELETE/PUT/rm-rf on real data
cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); is_exempt=$(echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '/tmp/|/var/folders/' && echo yes || echo no); if [ "$is_exempt" = "no" ] && echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'curl.*-X[[:space:]]+(DELETE|PUT)|rm[[:space:]]+-[rRf]+'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"RULE 010 BLOCK: DELETE/PUT/rm-rf with real IDs is FORBIDDEN. Use nonexistent/mock IDs for testing. 2026-04-06 incident: 9 workspaces were deleted."}}'; fi

# SSOT_RULE_020 — Block edit without prior read
fp=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); sid=$(jq -r '.session_id // "default"'); sf="/tmp/anuki-read-files-$sid"; if [ -z "$fp" ]; then exit 0; fi; if [ ! -f "$fp" ]; then exit 0; fi; if [ ! -f "$sf" ] || ! grep -qxF "$fp" "$sf" 2>/dev/null; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"RULE 020 BLOCK: You have not read this file yet. No assumptions — read first, then edit."}}'; fi

# SSOT_RULE_DYNAMIC — hardcoded count detection
input=$(cat)
ns=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // .tool_input.content // empty')
if echo "$ns" | grep -qE '===\s*(8|9|10|11|12)\s*[;,)\]]|agents\.length\s*===\s*[0-9]+|workspaces\.length\s*===\s*[0-9]+|count\s*===\s*[0-9]+'; then
  echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"DYNAMIC-FIRST BLOCK: Hardcoded count detected (=== fixed number). Query dynamically from API — never hardcode counts."}}'
fi

# SSOT_RULE_STACKPATCH — 5+ edits to same file detection
input=$(cat)
fp=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty')
sid=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.session_id // "default"')
ef="/tmp/anuki-edit-counts-$sid"
if [ -z "$fp" ]; then exit 0; fi
echo "$fp" >> "$ef"
count=$(grep -cxF "$fp" "$ef" 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ "$count" -ge 5 ]; then
  echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"STACK PATCH BLOCK: 5+ edits to the same file detected — risk of patch-on-patch. STOP. Find root cause, diff against last working state, revert if needed."}}'
fi

# SSOT_RULE_SDK — Anthropic SDK import detection
input=$(cat)
ns=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // .tool_input.content // empty')
if echo "$ns" | grep -qE '@anthropic-ai/sdk|from anthropic import|require\(.anthropic.\)|import Anthropic|new Anthropic'; then
  echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"SDK BLOCK: Anthropic SDK import is FORBIDDEN. This project uses Claude CLI spawn model. Do not suggest SDK migration."}}'
fi

# SSOT_STOP_AUDIT_005
BASEDIR=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." 2>/dev/null && pwd || echo "$PWD")
source "$BASEDIR/scripts/hook-helper.sh"
hook_stdin
check_hook_active
msg=$(get_last_message)
if [ -z "$msg" ]; then exit 0; fi
has_claim=$(echo "$msg" | grep -ciE "unused|dead code|not needed|does not exist|not found|can be deleted|can be removed|unnecessary" || true)
has_evidence=$(echo "$msg" | grep -cE "\.[a-z]{1,4}:[0-9]+|grep.*src/|verified|confirmed|PASS" || true)
if [ "$has_claim" -gt 0 ] && [ "$has_evidence" -eq 0 ]; then
  emit_block "RULE 005 AUDIT: Response contains unverified claims. Verify with evidence (file:line, grep, test results) and rewrite."
fi

# SSOT_STOP_BANDAID — bug fix without root cause analysis detection
BASEDIR=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." 2>/dev/null && pwd || echo "$PWD")
source "$BASEDIR/scripts/hook-helper.sh"
hook_stdin
check_hook_active
tp="$_HOOK_TRANSCRIPT_PATH"
if [ -z "$tp" ] || [ ! -f "$tp" ]; then exit 0; fi
has_fix=$(grep -ciE 'fix|bug|error|crash|broken|patch' "$tp" 2>/dev/null || true)
has_edit=$(grep -c '"tool_name":"Edit"\|"tool_name":"Write"' "$tp" 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ "$has_fix" -lt 2 ] || [ "$has_edit" -lt 1 ]; then exit 0; fi
has_rootcause=$(grep -ciE 'root.?cause|impact.?scan|same.?pattern|grep -r|grep -rn|codebase.*scan|other.?place|regression|guard|permanent fix' "$tp" 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ "$has_rootcause" -lt 1 ]; then
  emit_block "BAND-AID GUARD: Bug fix detected but no root cause analysis trace found. Find the root cause, scan codebase for same pattern (grep -r), add permanent guard. Band-aid fixes are FORBIDDEN."
fi
